body_txt: A major announcement is made more dramatic by being said in a matter-of-fact tone. Brevity, as the Real Life examples show, can be the soul of this trope. Ancient Sparta used it, making this Older Than Feudalism . To say that someone has a "Laconic Wit" is a reference to the Spartan tendency towards this; Sparta was (and is) located in the Greek region of Laconia. A Super-Trope to Little "No" , Flat "What" , and often Blunt "Yes" . Compare Tranquil Fury , Major Injury Underreaction , Danger Deadpan , This Is Gonna Suck , Dull Surprise (if and the facial expression is appropriate), Casual Danger Dialogue . Contrast Chewing the Scenery , Large Ham , Big Word Shout .
